Ian Watkins, the former lead singer of the rock band Lostprophets, was incarcerated, significantly impacting his career. He was sentenced to 29 years in prison in 2013 for multiple sexual offenses, leading to the immediate disbandment of Lostprophets.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lostprophets), [3](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [4](https://www.livenowfox.com/news/ian-watkins-lostprophets-crimes-prison-attack-dead), [5](https://www.theguardian.com/uk-news/2013/dec/18/ian-watkins-jailed-child-sex-crimes) Watkins later died in prison in 2025.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [3](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [4](https://www.livenowfox.com/news/ian-watkins-lostprophets-crimes-prison-attack-dead), [6](https://www.theguardian.com/uk-news/2025/oct/24/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-died-from-stab-wound-to-the-neck-inquest-told)

In contrast, Josey Scott, the former lead vocalist of the rock band Saliva, left the band in 2011 to pursue a solo Christian music career and to address personal struggles with drug and alcohol addiction.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josey_Scott), [8](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html) His departure from Saliva was not due to incarceration.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josey_Scott), [8](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html)
